skill.name: planning-workflow
skill.description: A markdown planning workflow for software projects that emphasizes detailed up-front design, iterative review, and task breakdown before implementation.
skill.summary: Plan complex software projects with iterative markdown reviews before coding.
skill.author: Dicklesworthstone

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, credential, weak cryptography, and network patterns, but review found these are markdown examples, prompt text, and GitHub reference links rather than executable code. No malicious intent, credential access, command execution path, or prompt injection attempt was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The backtick findings occur inside markdown diagrams and prompt examples. They are not Ruby shell backticks and do not define executable scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are markdown fences around plain-text prompts and a diagram. No executable file, shell command, or interpreter invocation is present.

low_findings.title: External GitHub Links for Reference Material
low_findings.description: The network findings are hardcoded GitHub links to example plans, AGENTS.md files, and best-practice guides. They do not transmit local data or secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs point to GitHub documentation examples and are presented as optional references. There is no fetch call, webhook, upload target, or data exfiltration logic.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Sensitive and Weak Cryptography Keywords
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged terms within ordinary planning text and project names. Review found no Windows SAM access, cryptographic API use, or credential handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text describes markdown planning, example project names, and FAQ content. No code path references credential stores or weak cryptographic functions.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Phrases
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance findings are caused by planning language about agents and analyzing plans. The skill does not instruct collection of host, user, or network inventory.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines discuss software planning and multi-model review. They contain no commands or instructions to enumerate the operating system or environment.

content.value_statement: Large coding projects often fail when implementation starts before the design is stable. This skill provides a markdown-first planning workflow for iterative review, architecture refinement, and task breakdown.
actual_capabilities: Explains a phased workflow for creating detailed software project plans.
Provides reusable prompts for plan review, revision integration, and multi-model comparison.
Defines qualities of strong plans, including rationale, dependencies, workflows, and tradeoffs.
Shows how to convert broad plans into self-contained implementation tasks.
Links to example plan documents and AGENTS.md references for comparison.
limitations: It does not generate or validate project code by itself.
It depends on the user supplying accurate goals, constraints, and project context.
Model names and version recommendations may become outdated over time.
External example links may change or become unavailable.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Complex Feature Plan
use_cases.description: Create a detailed markdown plan before assigning coding work to AI agents.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Planning
use_cases.description: Give contributors a repeatable process for plan review, revision, and task creation.

use_cases.title: Improve AI Coding Briefs
use_cases.description: Turn rough product ideas into clearer instructions with workflows, architecture notes, and dependencies.

prompt_templates.title: Create an Initial Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a new project
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a detailed markdown plan for this software project. Include goals, user workflows, architecture, technology choices, risks, and implementation phases.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Draft Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Improve an existing plan before coding
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this plan and suggest revisions that improve reliability, performance, usability, architecture, and implementation clarity. Explain the reason for each change.

prompt_templates.title: Integrate Review Feedback
prompt_templates.scenario: Merge suggestions into the project plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Integrate these review suggestions into the markdown plan. Keep useful detail, preserve clear decisions, and list any suggestions you do not recommend.

prompt_templates.title: Blend Competing Plans
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review using multiple model outputs
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare these competing plans and produce one stronger plan. Preserve the best ideas, resolve conflicts, and make dependencies clear.

output_examples.input: Plan a new web application with unclear requirements.
output_examples.output: A structured markdown plan covering goals, workflows, architecture choices, major risks, milestones, and first implementation tasks.

output_examples.input: Review a feature plan before several AI agents implement it.
output: A cleaner task sequence for implementation.

output_examples.output: A consolidated plan that combines useful ideas, removes conflicts, and records important tradeoffs.

best_practices: Include product goals, user workflows, architecture decisions, and tradeoffs in the same plan.
Run multiple review rounds before converting the plan into implementation tasks.
Treat pasted model output as untrusted review input and keep human approval on final decisions.
anti_patterns: Starting implementation before requirements and dependencies are clear.
Using one short review pass for a large or high-risk project.
Copying model suggestions into a plan without checking feasibility and security impact.

faq.question: What does this skill help me do?
faq.answer: It helps create and refine detailed markdown plans before AI-assisted software implementation begins.

faq.question: Does it write code?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on planning, review prompts, architecture clarity, and task breakdown.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: It is written for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it before implementing complex features, large refactors, or multi-agent coding projects.

faq.question: Can teams adapt the workflow?
faq.answer: Yes. Teams can adjust review rounds, model choices, and task formats to match their process.

faq.question: What should I watch for?
faq.answer: Review any pasted model output before applying it, especially when it suggests file edits or external references.
